title: leucanthemum vulgare subsp. gracilicaule
intro: Leucanthemum vulgare subsp. gracilicaule, also known as a subspecies of the common daisy, is a flowering plant that belongs to the daisy family (Asteraceae). This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional European folk medicine, where it has been used to treat various health issues due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Leucanthemum vulgare subsp. gracilicaule include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory problems, and soothe digestive issues. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
